heading: Support for Treaty in Victoria is strong and consistent
body: Support for Treaty is 40%, oppose 32%, neither 28% at the start of the survey (C5). After hearing arguments for and against, it holds at 40% support to 35% oppose (E1). Similar to the Victorian state segment in October 2025 (40 support / 35 oppose / 25 neither).

heading: The most effective arguments for Treaty
body: 48% rank "everyone deserves to be treated fairly, with dignity and respect — but for too long politicians have enforced unfair policies" in their top two. 47% rank "we want a country that values all people, whether they have been here five years, five generations or five thousand generations".

heading: Voters see Liberal policy as out of touch — and a One Nation policy
body: 58% of voters think scrapping the Treaty within 100 days is out of touch with their priorities (E2) — a majority across all electorate types, and higher in the Liberal Leader's seat of Kew, with 61% in Southern Metropolitan saying out of touch. 63% see Liberal treaty policy as a One Nation policy (E3), including 56% of intending Coalition voters.

heading: Voters reject racism
body: 45% say they have witnessed or experienced racism, and a majority of younger and tertiary-educated voters have. 74% say they would be less likely to vote for someone who made statements that were racist or encouraged racism towards a particular group.

heading: Top issues and party competence
body: Voters' top issues are managing the economy and crime; Treaty ranks 12th (C1). The Coalition is rated best at managing the economy and crime; on Treaty, "don't know" leads (C3).
